items.q: Which type of muscle is found only in the heart?
items.why: Cardiac muscle is unique to the heart and contracts automatically without conscious control.

items.q: Where in the lungs does gas exchange take place?
items.correct: In the alveoli, tiny air sacs surrounded by capillaries.
items.why: The huge surface area of the alveoli, close to capillaries, allows oxygen and carbon dioxide to diffuse.

items.q: Is cardiac muscle under conscious control?
items.correct: No - it is involuntary and contracts automatically.
items.misconception: Yes, you control each heartbeat consciously.
items.random_wrong: It only works when you exercise.
items.why: Cardiac muscle is myogenic, beating on its own without you having to think about it.

items.q: Which muscle type is found only in the heart?
items.why: It's Cardiac muscle. “Smooth muscle” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Where in the body does gas exchange take place?
items.why: It's Alveoli. “Trachea” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: How many bones are in the adult human skeleton?
items.why: Exact answer: 206. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
